source_jobs.title: Head of SEO and AI Visibility
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Smallpdf was founded in 2013 with one simple belief: working with documents shouldn’t be complicated. Today, we’re an industry leader in digital document solutions, trusted by millions of users across 195+ countries. We build intuitive, powerful tools that help individuals and businesses work smarter with their documents. From compressing and converting files to editing and e-signing, our platform makes complex document workflows simple, secure, and accessible.

As a product-led company headquartered in Switzerland and powered by a diverse, global team of 110+ people (across the group), we combine product excellence with a strong focus on user experience, data-driven decision-making and continuous innovation. We operate at the intersection of simplicity and performance, building scalable solutions that power everyday productivity for individuals and businesses alike. Our team is driven by ownership, experimentation and a shared commitment to delivering meaningful impact at scale.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Smallpdf is hiring a Head of SEO to own and execute our global organic search strategy. Reporting to our new VP of Growth, the Head of SEO will manage the SEO team, set and prioritize the SEO roadmap based on expected growth in qualified traffic and new ARR, and partner closely with Product and Engineering to ship key initiatives. You’ll also help shape our organic content strategy, improve our visibility in AI search/LLMs, and build a structured experimentation program to learn quickly and drive repeatable gains.

This role is for someone who can operate at both strategic and tactical levels: define direction, align stakeholders, and deliver measurable results via strong execution - including getting your hands dirty with the details.

The ideal candidate is highly data-driven and analytical, but able to find creative new opportunities for organic growth.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead Smallpdf’s global SEO program (technical, on-page, content, off-page) to grow organic visibility, relevant traffic, and new ARR from search, while improving discoverability in LLMs and AI-driven search.

 - Manage and mentor a team of 3-6 full-time and freelance SEO specialists. Set goals, development plans, and operating cadence (ideation, planning, execution, measurement and reviews). Foster a culture of rapid, high-quality execution and continuous learning.

 - Partner with the Head of Marketing to drive brand visibility in AI Search / LLMs.

 - Own Smallpdf’s SEO roadmap, linking priorities to: A) business outcomes (e.g. new ARR), B) SEO metrics (e.g. rankings, traffic, backlinks), and C) LLMO metrics (e.g. brand visibility, citations, mentions).

 - Collaborate strongly with Product and Engineering to execute our SEO roadmap.

 - Align stakeholders across the Leadership team, Marketing, and Product and Engineering to ensure SEO and LLMO priorities are properly understood and resourced. Report back on results with clear data, action plan, and asks.

 - Design, implement and iterate experiments to improve SEO performance at a rigorous pace. Build a standard operating procedure for SEO experiments including hypothesis, prioritization, measurement, rollout and reporting.

 - Help shape Smallpdf’s content strategy to align with SEO and LLMO opportunities. Provide input on optimizing content operations (target keywords, content templates, refresh cycles, competitive research) to expand our organic footprint while maintaining quality.

 - Define and track SEO KPIs, running recurring audits and monitoring to ensure we remain on-track and mitigate risk.




ABOUT YOU

 - 6–10 years of SEO experience including technical SEO, on-page, content, and off-page (ideally 2+ years in product-led SaaS)

 - 2+ years leading a team, with a track record of developing high-performing SEO contributors

 - Strong knowledge of modern search ranking systems and quality guidelines, with a track record of maintaining performance through algorithm updates

 - Exper

source_jobs.title: Data Scientist: Product & Analytics
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SMALLPDF

Smallpdf was founded in 2013 with one simple belief: working with documents shouldn't be complicated.

What started as an easier way to compress PDFs has grown into one of Europe's largest product-led technology companies. Today, more than 17 million people use Smallpdf every month, our products have been used by over 1.1 billion users across 195 countries, and every day we power millions of document workflows - from PDF conversion and editing to e-signing and AI-powered document tools.

Our success came from rethinking an established market. Instead of building complex software for experts, we broke document management down into simple jobs to be done - compress a PDF, merge files, convert documents, collect signatures - and made those experiences intuitive for everyone. That product philosophy continues to shape how we build today.

For a Data Scientist, this creates an unusually interesting environment.

Every month, millions of users interact with our products across acquisition channels, pricing, subscriptions, experimentation, product adoption, search, and AI-powered experiences. Every feature launch, monetization decision, and product improvement generates signals at a scale that allows us to test ideas, validate hypotheses, and continuously improve the user experience through data.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This isn't a traditional Data Scientist role. You won't spend your days optimizing machine learning models in notebooks or publishing research. Instead, you'll own analytical products that directly influence how millions of users experience Smallpdf.

You'll work across Product, Growth and Engineering to:

 - design experiments

 - improve decision making

 - own production analytical systems

 - build reliable data products

 - uncover opportunities hidden in data

 - translate complex analyses into business impact

You'll be joining a small, highly autonomous team where everyone owns outcomes rather than tasks.

If you're looking for a role where you can combine engineering, statistics, product thinking and business impact, you'll enjoy this.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Own production data products

You'll maintain and evolve analytical systems that directly power business decisions, including our marketing automation pipeline and core semantic data models.

Design and analyze experiments

Work closely with Product and Growth teams to design experiments, define success metrics, analyze outcomes and recommend next steps.

Build reliable analytical foundations

Develop dbt models, improve data quality, monitor production pipelines and ensure stakeholders trust the data they use every day.

Solve ambiguous business problems

You'll rarely receive perfectly defined tasks. Instead, you'll partner with stakeholders to understand problems, explore data, identify opportunities and propose solutions before anyone asks.

Turn data into decisions

Communicate insights clearly to technical and non-technical audiences, helping teams prioritize what matters most.

Continuously improve our data platform

You'll work with Data Engineers to improve our analytical infrastructure, semantic layer, experimentation framework, as well as future AI evaluation systems.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

You probably have

 - Several years of experience as a Data Scientist in a modern product company.

 - Strong Python and SQL skills.

 - Experience building production data products that drove real business impact - not just notebooks.

 - Solid understanding of experimentation, statistics and hypothesis testing.

 - Experience working with modern data warehouses and transformation tools (dbt is a plus, not a requirement).

 - Experience partnering directly with Product, Growth or Marketing teams.


YOU'LL STAND OUT IF YOU

 - Love understanding products and users.

 - Naturally investigate anomalies instead of ignoring them.

 - Enjoy solving messy, ambiguous problems.

 - Care about shipping useful work rather than perfect work.

 - Can expla

source_jobs.title: Data Analyst: Growth & Product
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Smallpdf was founded in 2013 with one simple belief: working with documents shouldn’t be complicated. Today, we’re an industry leader in digital document solutions, trusted by millions of users across 195+ countries. We build intuitive, powerful tools that help individuals and businesses work smarter with their documents. From compressing and converting files to editing and e-signing, our platform makes complex document workflows simple, secure, and accessible.

As a product-led company headquartered in Switzerland and powered by a diverse, global team of 110+ people (across the group), we combine product excellence with a strong focus on user experience, experimentation, and data-informed decision-making. Every month, more than 60 million people interact with our products across SEO, paid acquisition, partnerships, subscription flows, monetization funnels, and product experiences - creating a uniquely rich and complex growth ecosystem to understand and improve.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a expert Growth & Product Analyst to become a high-leverage analytical partner to our Product and Marketing teams.

This is not a reporting ownership role. It’s not a data science role either.

This role exists to help Smallpdf answer difficult growth and product questions that don’t have obvious answers:

 - How much incremental value are our acquisition channels driving?

 - Which product behaviors meaningfully predict monetization?

 - Where are we losing potential long-term customers?

 - What are the key levers that drive conversion?

 - What’s the best experiment design for a change with no real control group?

You’ll work across the full user journey - from acquisition (SEO, paid search, direct, partnerships) to activation, conversion, retention, and monetization.

One week you might investigate how much incremental new ARR paid search is truly driving vs. cannibalizing from SEO. The next, you could be figuring out what’s causing a dip in conversion rate to paying customers in Spain.

Some questions won’t have clean answers. Some analyses will challenge assumptions the business already believes. That’s part of the job.

We’re not looking for someone who waits for perfectly defined questions with clear guidance of exactly how to investigate them.

We’re looking for someone who sees patterns others miss, becomes obsessed with understanding them, and helps the business make better decisions because of it.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own deep, high-impact investigations and analyses across Product and Marketing

 - Analyze the full acquisition → activation → conversion → retention funnel

 - Investigate questions around:
   
   - incrementality
   
   - attribution and cannibalization
   
   - monetization behavior
   
   - funnel leakage
   
   - growth efficiency

 - Partner closely with Marketing leaders and Product Managers leaders, providing insights for business-critical decisions

 - Proactively identify anomalies, behavioral patterns, and hidden growth opportunities

 - Translate complex analyses into clear takeaways and recommendations

 - Support experimentation by helping define success metrics, evaluating impact, and interpreting results - including in cases where clean control groups aren’t possible (e.g. SEO)

 - Contribute to improving metric clarity and analytical consistency across teams

 - Collaborate with Data Engineering and Data Science teams when deeper technical implementation is required




ABOUT YOU

 - You’re a strong data analyst with 4+ years of experience, including in growth or marketing analytics

 - You have hands-on experience with:
   
   - SQL
   
   - incrementality analysis
   
   - conversion funnel analysis
   
   - A/B experimentation
   
   - attribution tracking

 - You’re comfortable with ambiguity and incomplete data. You know that some of the most important business questions can’t be answered perfectly - but still need to be answered thoughtfully.

 - You can independently s

notes: Smallpdf is a product-led company with a strong focus on digital document solutions.
